scope.rationale: scope_override:cds_support accepted. Override rationale: The component calculates a warning score from clinical observations and creates deterioration escalation actions, so clinical safety and decision-support assurance are mandatory. CSAA automatic classification was out_of_scope.

hazards.title: Wrong-patient context bleed across sessions
hazards.description: Patient demographic or clinical summary cached across sessions displays prior patient's record to a new user (shared workstation, tab reuse, stale JWT).
hazards.clinical_effect: Clinical action (prescribe / note / refer) recorded against the wrong patient, causing treatment error.
additional_controls: Persistent patient banner and two-identifier medication verification

hazards.title: Stale clinical data propagation
hazards.description: Cached chart data served after the upstream system of record updates, causing the clinician to act on obsolete allergies / medications / results.
hazards.clinical_effect: Prescribing against an outdated allergy list leads to anaphylaxis; acting on stale results delays diagnosis.
additional_controls: No upstream cache or offline fallback exists in Phase 1
Phase 2 freshness and provenance contract is explicit

hazards.title: Unavailable downstream service with unsafe fallback
hazards.description: When consent-service / terminology / patient-registry is unavailable, the app continues with a permissive default instead of failing closed.
hazards.clinical_effect: Patient data accessed without valid consent; wrong medication dispensed because SNOMED lookup fell back to string match.
additional_controls: Phase 1 exposes no downstream clinical-service client or fallback
Health evidence reports integrations as not implemented

hazards.title: Silent truncation of clinical text
hazards.description: Clinical notes, allergy descriptions, or medication instructions truncated at the backend storage layer without surfacing to the user.
hazards.clinical_effect: Key clinical information (e.g. 'NOT to be given with warfarin') lost; subsequent clinician misses contra-indication.
additional_controls: Declared Pydantic input limits reject oversized text before storage

hazards.title: Unresolvable duplicate identifier
hazards.description: Two patient records map to the same NHI due to data-entry error OR two NHIs for the same patient due to demographic drift.
hazards.clinical_effect: Records merged or split incorrectly, losing history; clinician acts on partial timeline.
existing_controls: manual merge requires clinical safety review
additional_controls: No probabilistic identity merge exists in Phase 1
Medication workflow verifies MRN and date of birth

hazards.title: Audit-chain break undetected
hazards.description: The per-patient or per-actor tamper-evident audit chain breaks (hash mismatch) without alerting the CSO.
hazards.clinical_effect: Regulatory investigation (GMC / HCPC / GPhC) cannot verify sequence of actions; potential patient-safety incident cannot be reconstructed.
existing_controls: immediate CSO page on detected break
additional_controls: Database triggers reject audit updates and deletes
Health and restricted audit endpoints verify the hash chain

hazards.title: Alert fatigue suppresses safety alerts
hazards.description: Clinicians habitually override alerts due to high false-positive rate, so genuine critical alerts are also overridden without consideration.
hazards.clinical_effect: Patient harm from missed allergy / interaction / contraindication despite the CDS engine correctly firing an alert.
causes: no differentiation between information / warning / hard-stop
additional_controls: Only threshold-crossing observations create owned escalation tasks
No batch dismiss or alert override exists

hazards.title: Model drift degrades prediction quality
hazards.description: The deployed ML / risk-scoring model's prediction quality degrades over time as the patient population or clinical practice evolves.
hazards.clinical_effect: Systematic under-prediction of high-risk patients; late escalation; patient harm.
additional_controls: Version-controlled deterministic scoring has normal and critical regression tests

release_blockers: The canonical intended-use statement requires human CSO approval.
Human CSO approval is not evidenced by this generated draft.
Release-authority and top-management acceptance are not evidenced.
Required DPIA completion and DPO approval are not evidenced.
Required MHRA review is not evidenced.
